content: Go to Settings > General > Incoming Mails and click on the "App Icon Options" button to open the pop-up window in which you can select your preferred option for the red dot in the Thunderbird icon in the Dock

content: That screenshot you are referring to, is exactly the Match Field option that is not being offered to rectify the problem on subsequent tries. See if you can get that to happen after your initial try; go back and do it the 2nd time and see if you can. And just because you test only did it the one time and not any subsequent time then that when you can see the problem yourself. Just to explain again because you keep on bringing up the Outlook option; please try the CSV file option and make sure your CSV file is not, I repeat not in Thunderbird CSV file order. The CSV file option hopefully can handle any CSV file order from any Address Book import not just Thunderbird. An just to be concise. The profile on the Outlook import is required to make the import work. Which is how you got Your Screenshot. And to make sure we are on the same page, I hope the Thunderbird import of a CSV file does not have to be in Thunderbird import field order or that would make the CSV option useless. But just to be even clearer even if the CSV file order was close but not exact. The 2nd and subsequent attempts won't allow the Match field screen to correct the Field order.
creator: ghbgiest

content: If the toolbars are not shown, press the alt key. to regain the send function, click 'new message' button, then click alt key to shown menu bar and then tick view>toolbars and tick the cocmposition toolbar.
creator: davidsk

content: You could send a message from Thunderbird to the Obamas if you had an e-mail address for them. But you have an address for a contact form on a web page. That is not an e-mail address. The Obamas want you to fill out the form in a web browser and submit it. That will send them an e-mail message. No role for Thunderbird here.
creator: mozilla98
